name: Witten effect (theta-induced monopole electric charge)
summary: Witten (1979) showed that in a non-Abelian gauge theory with vacuum angle theta, a magnetic monopole of magnetic charge g acquires an electric charge q = -e theta / (2 pi) (in addition to integer charges from CP-symmetric quantization). The result links the QCD-vacuum theta angle to fractional electric charges on grand-unified-theory monopoles, and provides a window into axion-monopole interactions: as the axion field a relaxes, the induced electric charge oscillates, leading to phenomenologically detectable axion-monopole dynamics. Underlies modern monopole-search strategies and the 'monopole-induced axion energy loss' bound on axion-monopole coupling.

addresses_puzzles.note: Provides a separate observational handle on theta_QCD: monopoles (if they exist) would carry electric charge q = -e theta / (2 pi). Combined with bounds on millicharged-monopole abundances, this gives an independent (though loose) bound on theta. Theoretical relevance > experimental relevance currently.

touches_benchmarks.note: The Witten effect is consistent with theta < 10^-10 from neutron-EDM bounds; monopole-induced electric charges would be ~ 10^-11 e for GUT monopoles, far below current detection thresholds.

introduces.note: No new parameters - a derived consequence of the theta-vacuum structure of non-Abelian gauge theory. Predicts a relationship q = -e theta / (2 pi) for magnetic monopoles.

history: Witten (1979), 'Dyons of charge e theta / 2 pi', Phys. Lett. B 86, 283. Foundational result interpreted in many GUT and string-theoretic contexts. Modern axion-monopole literature: Fischler-Giedt-Tabakov (1988); Sokolov-Ringwald (2022, arXiv:2205.02605) for axion-monopole oscillation in laboratory contexts.

references.citation: Witten, 'Dyons of charge e theta / 2 pi', Phys. Lett. B 86, 283 (1979)
